task_completion.description: The dashboard is created and named 'Canary v2 Validation'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the dashboard title 'Canary v2 Validation' at the top of the Graphite web interface.

task_completion.description: A graph titled 'CPU Regression Checker' is present with the required aliased targets.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph with the title 'CPU Regression Checker'. The legend below or inside the graph must show exactly three aliases: 'Stable CPU', 'Canary CPU', and 'CPU Penalty'.

task_completion.description: A graph titled 'Disk I/O Multiplier' is present with the required aliased target.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph with the title 'Disk I/O Multiplier'. The legend must show the alias 'Canary I/O Ratio'.

task_completion.description: A graph titled 'Cumulative CPU Cost' is present with the required aliased targets.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph with the title 'Cumulative CPU Cost'. The legend must show the aliases 'Cumulative Stable CPU' and 'Cumulative Canary CPU'.

task_completion.description: The dashboard has been successfully saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Dashboard Saved' notification, or check if the browser URL reflects the saved dashboard name (e.g., /dashboard/Canary%20v2%20Validation) rather than a new unsaved state.

integrity.description: The agent used the Graphite web interface to construct the dashboard.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Graphite browser UI (e.g., adding graphs, editing metrics, applying functions like diffSeries, divideSeries, and integral) rather than just writing a JSON file in a terminal or text editor.

integrity.description: The graphs display actual data lines.
integrity.visual_evidence: The graphs should show plotted lines or curves representing the real EC2 metrics, not empty charts or error messages, proving the metrics were correctly queried.

integrity.description: The agent did not mock the results in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the final result is a functional Graphite dashboard in the browser, not a text file containing the expected graph titles and aliases.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected dashboard name is exactly 'Canary v2 Validation'. It must contain exactly three graphs with the titles: 'CPU Regression Checker', 'Disk I/O Multiplier', and 'Cumulative CPU Cost'. The data used is real-world EC2 CPU utilization and disk write metrics, so the graphs should display actual plotted data lines, not empty charts.
